name: Check Cashing Of Raleigh
description_short: Check Cashing of Raleigh provides check cashing, money orders, bill payments, and money transfers to residents across the Triangle area. Serves payroll, government, and tax checks with no holds.
description_long:
Check Cashing of Raleigh is an established financial services provider located at 1100 Raleigh Blvd in Raleigh, North Carolina. The company has evolved from a basic check-cashing operation into a multi-service financial access point serving individuals and businesses across the Triangle region, including Raleigh, Garner, Wendell, Cary, Morrisville, Knightdale, Wake Forest, Apex, Holly Springs, Fuquay Varina, and surrounding areas.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of financial services beyond check cashing. Their core services include cashing payroll checks, government checks, and tax checks without holds or daily cash maximums. They also provide money orders (up to $500), Western Union money transfers to domestic and international recipients, bill payments for utilities and various service providers, and prepaid phone cards from major carriers including AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon. Additional services include money transfer options, bill payment facilitation, stamps, envelopes, photocopying, and faxing.

Check Cashing of Raleigh distinguishes itself through extended business hours (Mon-Fri 8am-8pm, Sat 8am-7pm), a strict policy requiring all checks to be signed in-store in front of employees, and service to a broad geographic area. Their website emphasizes "quick, convenient and personal solutions" and markets competitively with phrases like "Best Deals Guaranteed." They maintain Spanish-language resources and operate an authorized bill payment center.

The company operates as a traditional check-cashing service without apparent regulatory oversight information or transparent fee disclosure on their website. While they serve an underbanked population, customers should verify specific fees and compare rates before using their services, as check-cashing businesses typically charge percentage-based fees that vary by check type and amount.
pros: Extended business hours (Mon-Fri 8am-8pm, Sat 8am-7pm) accommodate working customers
No holds on checks and no daily cash maximums for payroll, government, and tax checks
Broad geographic service area covering 10+ Triangle region communities
Multiple check types accepted (payroll, government, tax) with low advertised fees
Western Union money transfer partnership enables domestic and international cash transfers
One-stop shop for check cashing, bill payments, money orders, and prepaid phone cards
cons: No transparent fee schedule published on website; customers must visit in-person to learn exact rates
Strict policy requiring all checks to be signed in-store limits convenience for some customers
No online or mobile check-cashing capability; requires in-person visit
Money orders capped at $500 maximum per order
Single physical location limits accessibility despite serving 10+ surrounding communities
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked workers in the Raleigh Triangle area needing immediate access to payroll
People without traditional bank accounts requiring bill payment and money transfer services
Customers serving the surrounding Triangle communities seeking extended evening and Saturday hours
Spanish-speaking residents needing bilingual financial services
diagnosis: Check Cashing of Raleigh is best for unbanked workers and residents in the Triangle area who need immediate access to payroll funds, bill payment services, or money transfers without a traditional bank account. The main caveat is that fees are not transparently posted online, requiring in-person visits to compare costs, and the company's single location may limit convenience despite serving a geographically broad customer base.
services: Western Union money transfers (domestic and international)
Bill payments (utilities, cable, credit cards, mortgages)
Prepaid phone cards (AT&T, T-Mobile, Verizon, TracFone, Boost, others)
